title: GSC Data Pipeline Emergency Fix — Phase 20.7
root_cause: gsc-daily-pipeline.py had HARDCODED date ranges (startDate=2026-05-31, endDate=2026-06-07). This is why every daily run since June 7 returned the same stale 499-impression baseline.

dynamic_date_ranges.before: Hardcoded: startDate='2026-05-31', endDate='2026-06-07' on lines 33-34 and 67-68
dynamic_date_ranges.after: Dynamic: end_date = today - 3 days (GSC delay). start_date = end_date - 6 days. Updated every run.

row_limit.before: 250 (risk of silent data loss per pitfall #63)

checksum_tracking.before: None — no way to detect staleness
checksum_tracking.after: MD5 checksum computed and saved: dfd0d4d55880

correction_factor.after: DISABLED — correction factor stored as documentation only. Corrected estimate labeled FYI only.

freshness_proof.after: freshness field: {fp['freshness']}. Compares date range and checksum to previous pull.

28_day_window.after: 28-day metrics included: 1150 imp, 1 clicks, 1 pages with clicks

test_result.output: API RAW: 424 imp, 0 clicks, 51 pages, pos 38.3

manual_ui_data.section: FYI ONLY — separate from official KPIs
